case_title: MICHAEL GIPSON VS. IMO INDUSTRIES LLC ET AL

docket_entries.description: SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 74283214), PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF GIPSON, MICHAEL SERVED SEP-09-2024, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT REPUBLIC SUPPLY COMPANY

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (TRANSACTION ID # 76538094) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GIPSON, MICHAEL HEARING SET FOR JUL-29-2025 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 304

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 36(A). NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE TRIAL DATE IS NOVEMBER 17, 2025, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS NOVEMBER 26, 2025. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS NOVEMBER 12, 2025. BEFORE A PARTY FILES AND SERVES A SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTION, IT MUST CONTACT THE CLERK TO MAKE A RESERVATION. THE FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS OCTOBER 31, 2025. THE EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS NOVEMBER 14, 2025. ALL BANKRUPTCY DOCUMENTS SHALL BE TURNED OVER TO DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN AUGUST 28, 2025. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR SEPTEMBER 30, 2025, AT 4:00 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 13, 2025, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. PERSONAL APPEARANCE IS REQUIRED BY ALL UNLESS A PARTY IS EXCUSED FOR GOOD CAUSE. A REQUEST TO EXCUSE A PERSONAL APPEARANCE MUST BE SUBMITTED TO DEPARTMENT503@SFTC.ORG TWO WEEKS BEFORE THE MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY. REPORTER: NOT REPORTED (304/EPS).
ASBESTOS MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR NOV-13-2025 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 503, PER ORDER FILED JULY 29, 2025 (503).

ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT CALENDAR OF NOV-13-2025 IS OFF CALENDAR - VACATED. COURT IS UNAVAILABLE FOR MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. PLAINTIFF IS NOTIFIED BY EMAIL CORRESPONDENCE. PLAINTIFF TO GIVE NOTICE TO ALL PARTIES. (503)

calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Continued To Jul-02-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 502

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Jan-27-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day, January 27, 2026 at 3:30 pm for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Roberts Bros. Construction Co.

Dept. 304. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Edward Santos, Reporter: NOT RECORDED (FTR). (304)

No Appearance by any party or counsel. Not contested.

After considering the written evidence presented and good cause appearing, the court adopts it's tentative ruling in favor of the Plaintiffs as follows:

The Application of Plaintiff Michael Gipson for Entry of Default Against Defendant Roberts Bros. Construction Co. is the amount of $2,000,000.00 in non-economic damages is GRANTED.

The Court has reviewed the prove-up packet including the Judicial Council Form CIV-100, the memorandum, Mr. Nevin's declaration and the attached exhibits, which are admitted. The Court finds Defendant has been served with process, failed to answer or appear and defend the action within the time allowed by law, and default has been duly entered. As such, Plaintiff is entitled to the relief sought in the Application. The Court intends to execute the proposed judgment accompanying the application.

Roberts Bros. Construction Co.
Non-Economic Damages - $2,000,000.00
Total - $2,000,000.00

Proposed Judgment was signed by the Court - see scanned Judgment document for details.

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Set For Trial On Nov-17-25 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 29, 2025, in Department 304, Line 1.
No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed.
1. The trial date is November 17, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is November 26, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is November 12, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is October 31, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is November 14, 2025.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than August 28, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(mini-minutes 1 of 2 continues, see next entry)

PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE
IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF - FOR TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (mini-minutes 2 of 2)

8. No later than August 12, 2025:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.
9. A further case management conference is set for September 30, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.
10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than October 6, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due October 20, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on October 27, 2025.
11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, November 13, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference. =(EPS/304).
